headlineSummary: HN sentiment was mixed-neutral for both Anthropic and OpenAI, with Anthropic showing limited evidence split between praise for Claude’s technical help and criticism of customer-hostile business moves [E1][E3], while OpenAI drew both strong criticism over PR and leadership chaos and praise for its brand and coding tools [E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic’s sentiment was mixed-neutral with limited evidence: one comment praised Claude for helping add useful Ghidra extensions, while another criticized the company’s customer-hostile strategy as unsustainable [E1][E3].

OpenAI’s sentiment was mixed-neutral, with one comment warning that negative PR and leadership chaos could hurt it badly, and another saying it has the better brand, consumer presence, and coding app/models [E2][E3].

evidence.excerpt: I've had Claude add extensions into Ghidra for Xbox/Wii specific instruction support

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Claude with successfully adding useful Ghidra extensions, indicating capable/helpful performance.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI thinks they're still competing on technology, and not user experience and market reputation otherwise they'd understand the continuous negative PR generated by Altman's chaos is going to cost them everything.

annotations.rationale: This directly criticizes OpenAI's strategy and reputation, saying its negative PR and leadership chaos will be severely damaging.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI is the better company (transparency, open sourcing things, how they handle things in general e.g. OpenClaw, how they compete, etc.) and they have the vastly better brand, the better consumer presence, and (for me and many others) they have the better coding app + models. Anthropic doing deeply customer hostile stuff - again and again - to produce a short term revenue spike does NOT make for a long-term sustainable business.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says OpenAI is the better company with better brand, consumer presence, and coding app/models.
The commenter calls Anthropic's behavior deeply customer hostile and unsustainable, a strong business criticism.
